kelp-bass.cast: Tight to boiler rock & kelp edges, 20–40 ft; fan the up-current edge

rigs.name: Weedless paddletail swimbait
rigs.detail: 5–7" Big Hammer, MC Swimbait, or Kicker Fish on a 1/2–3/4 oz Owner Beast or Gamakatsu Superline weedless hook. Burn it over the kelp; don't swing on the bite — keep grinding till it loads.

rigs.name: Leadhead swimbait
rigs.detail: 5" Big Hammer on a 1/2–1 oz Owner Ultrahead; count it down along rock edges and vary the sink. White/chartreuse in low light, natural in clear water.

rigs.name: Surface iron
rigs.detail: Tady 45 or Salas 6X Jr. in chrome/blue-white; burn it across the shallowest part of the reef at first light with a fast, erratic retrieve.

barred-sand-bass.cast: Fan-cast sandy flats & reef edges; retrieve near bottom

regs.season: Year-round · spawning aggregation closures may apply

rigs.name: Leadhead swimbait, slow-rolled
rigs.detail: 4–5" Big Hammer or MC Swimbait on a 1/2–3/4 oz Owner Ultrahead; stop-and-drop along the bottom. Natural/smoke colors in clear water, white or chartreuse on overcast days.

rigs.name: Carolina-rigged plastic
rigs.detail: 3/4–1 oz sliding sinker, #2 Owner hook, Roboworm or Yamamoto grub dragged over sand/mud flats. Green pumpkin and watermelon are reliable.

rigs.name: Flylined live squid or anchovy
rigs.detail: Hook squid through the mantle or anchovy through the nose on a 2/0 Owner SSW; drift near structure during the summer spawn.

spotted-bay-bass.cast: To pilings, eelgrass edges & channel drop-offs; work slow near bottom

rigs.name: Leadhead swimbait / dropshot
rigs.detail: 3–5" Big Hammer or Z-Man Swimmerz on a 3/16–3/8 oz Gamakatsu round head, or dropshot with a Roboworm straight tail. Reaction bites on the fall around pilings — let it hit bottom.
rigs.line: 15 lb Sufix braid → 12 lb Seaguar fluoro

rigs.name: Curly-tail grub
rigs.detail: AA Worms or Big Hammer 3" grub on a 1/8 oz Gamakatsu round head; motor oil, green pumpkin, or white depending on water clarity. Rotate the bait when they chew it off.

rigs.name: Ned rig / small crank
rigs.detail: Z-Man TRD or Yamamoto Senko Jr. on a 1/16 oz Ned head around dock shade and current breaks. Lucky Craft Pointer 78 minnow crank when they want something bigger.

largemouth-bass.cast: Points, submerged timber & windward bank; red-clay cliffs in 18–25 ft midday

regs.bag: 5/day state limit · Irvine Lake: catch-and-release only per lake rules

rigs.name: Wacky / Texas Senko
rigs.detail: 4–5" Yamamoto Senko wacky-rigged on a Gamakatsu G-Finesse hook, or Texas-rigged with a 1/8 oz bullet sinker around fallen timber. Green pumpkin and watermelon red are the go-to colors at Irvine.

rigs.name: Dropshot, Roboworm
rigs.detail: Roboworm 4.5" Straight Tail on a #1 Gamakatsu dropshot hook, 12–18" leader off a 3/16 oz weight; work slowly off the red-clay cliff edges at 18–25 ft during bright daytime.

rigs.name: Spinnerbait / topwater
rigs.detail: White 1/2 oz Strike King Premier Plus spinnerbait at dawn along the bank, or Heddon Zara Spook / Lucky Craft Sammy 100 on top after dark. Bass are catch-and-release at Irvine.

spotfin-croaker.cast: Into the first trough/gutter, 20–50 ft, on a building high

rigs.name: Carolina rig, sand crab
rigs.detail: 1/2 oz egg sinker, Mustad #4 beak hook, fresh soft-shell sand crab in the first trough. Soft mouth — feel for the weight before setting. Best bait by far during summer.

rigs.name: High-low surf rig, mussels
rigs.detail: Two Mustad #4 dropper loops baited with fresh blue mussels or ghost shrimp; covers two depths and doubles your shot at a bite.

rigs.name: Berkley Gulp! Sandworm
rigs.detail: 2" Gulp! Sandworm in natural or camo on a #6 Gamakatsu hook when sand crabs are scarce. Scent does the work.

california-corbina.cast: Sight-cast: lead cruising fish 6–10 ft, drop into the whitewash

rigs.name: Light Carolina, live sand crab
rigs.detail: Live soft-shell sand crab on a #2–4 Gamakatsu Octopus hook with a 1/4 oz egg sinker. Lead the corbina 6 ft, pitch into the whitewater, and don't move it — let the surf tumble the crab naturally.

rigs.name: Fly-lined sand crab, no weight
rigs.detail: Same hook, no sinker; let line out on the outgoing tide so the crab drifts away from the beach in the foam. Deadly technique.

rigs.name: Berkley Gulp! Sandworm
rigs.detail: 2" Gulp! Sandworm in camo or blood-red when sand crabs thin out in winter. Corbina rarely hit hard plastics but they'll eat this.

pacific-bonito.cast: Long casts into surface boils at the harbor mouth, 60–100 ft

rigs.name: Chrome cast jig
rigs.detail: 1 oz Luhr-Jensen Krocodile or Tady 45 in chrome/blue; fast erratic retrieve through the boil. Change angles when they stop biting — bonito spook easily after a few casts.

rigs.name: Flylined sardine or anchovy
rigs.detail: Live bait nose-hooked on a 1/0 Owner SSW; drift into a working school with minimal weight. Bonito bite best on the lightest possible line.

rigs.name: Iron jig or feather jig
rigs.detail: Salas 6X Jr. or a white/blue feather jig trolled slowly or cast to breaking fish. Also works as a run-and-gun option when the school moves fast.

pacific-mackerel.cast: Drop straight down off pier/jetty pilings, or short casts to the school

rigs.name: Hayabusa sabiki rig, tipped
rigs.detail: Size 4–6 Hayabusa sabiki with small squid tips on each hook; drop to the school and jig steadily. Best bait-maker at first light. Rinse and re-use the rig.

rigs.name: Small chrome jig
rigs.detail: 1/2 oz Krocodile or Lucky Joe in chrome or blue-white; fan-cast and retrieve fast when the school is busting on top.

rigs.name: Cut squid under a float
rigs.detail: 1" strip of squid on a #6 Gamakatsu hook suspended under a small bobber 3–5 ft down; hands-off approach that works all day from the pier.

barred-surfperch.cast: Into the trough just beyond the breakers, 20–40 ft; fish the foam seam

rigs.name: Carolina rig, sand crab
rigs.detail: Live or fresh sand crab on a #4–6 Gamakatsu Octopus hook with a 1/2 oz sliding egg sinker; drift through the trough on the incoming. The single best surfperch bait in SoCal.
rigs.line: 10–15 lb Ande or braid → 12 lb Seaguar fluoro

rigs.name: Hi-low dropper rig
rigs.detail: Two #4 Mustad droppers with sand crab or 2" Berkley Gulp! Sandworm; great for covering two depths to locate schooled fish quickly.

rigs.name: Grub on a light leadhead
rigs.detail: 2" motor-oil or root-beer Z-Man GrubZ on a 1/8 oz Gamakatsu round head cast into the wash and retrieved slowly through the foam.

opaleye.cast: Short casts into rocky wash zones & tidepool edges; berley the spot

rigs.name: Float rig, frozen peas
rigs.detail: Small Thill or Drennan float with a #8 Gamakatsu hook baited with 2–3 frozen peas. Chum the wash zone first — toss a handful of peas to draw fish up before presenting your hook.

rigs.name: Light dropper, ghost shrimp
rigs.detail: Fresh or frozen ghost shrimp on a fine-wire #8 Gamakatsu Hook tight to the rocks; also works for other reef species sharing the same zone.

rigs.name: Berkley Gulp! or innkeeper worm
rigs.detail: Small piece of Gulp! in natural or a live innkeeper worm on a #8 hook when they're keyed on chum and movement.

california-sheephead.cast: To reef edges & crevices; let it sit on the bottom, 20–50 ft

rigs.name: Dropper-loop, whole shrimp
rigs.detail: 1–2 oz sinker, #1 Owner SSW hook with a whole fresh shrimp, mussel, or rock crab parked on the bottom near structure. Big canine teeth crush shellfish — no need to finesse it.

rigs.name: Live or cut squid on leadhead
rigs.detail: Fresh squid on a 1/2 oz Gamakatsu round head dropped into crevices — one of the most consistent sheephead baits year-round.

rigs.name: Berkley Gulp! shrimp
rigs.detail: 3" Gulp! Shrimp in natural or pink on a #1 hook fished right on the reef bottom; holds scent longer than cut bait in current.

black-crappie.cast: To brush, docks & timber; suspend over the school and hold depth

rigs.name: Mini-jig under a float
rigs.detail: 1/16 oz Bobby Garland Baby Shad or Strike King Mr. Crappie tube in white or glow; suspend under a small Thill float at the school depth. Floating lantern at night concentrates fish fast.

rigs.name: Live minnow / micro-swimbait
rigs.detail: 1.5" minnow under a slip float, or a Z-Man 2" MinnowZ on a 1/32 oz head — crappie are visual feeders and key on small baitfish profiles.

rigs.name: Tiny spinner / ultra-light crank
rigs.detail: Blakemore Road Runner 1/16 oz or Rapala Ultra-Light Minnow slow-rolled past brush piles and dock posts.

channel-catfish.cast: To the flats, west shore & dam; soak on the bottom, 30–70 ft out

rigs.name: Slip-sinker bottom rig, cut mackerel
rigs.detail: 1–2 oz egg sinker, 18" leader, 2/0 Mustad Demon circle hook with fresh-cut mackerel or bonito on the bottom. The classic Irvine cat setup — evening and night produce best.

rigs.name: Nightcrawler / chicken liver
rigs.detail: Whole nightcrawler or chicken liver on a 1/0 Gamakatsu Octopus circle hook on a sliding sinker rig; versatile and effective even during trout season.

rigs.name: Magic Bait or Catmando stink bait
rigs.detail: Dip bait on a Catfish Charlie treble sponge hook; strong scent trail draws cats in murky water or after dark.

california-halibut.cast: Fan-cast sand flats & channel edges; drag slowly, 30–60 ft

rigs.name: Carolina rig, swimbait or live bait
rigs.detail: 1/2–3/4 oz sliding sinker, 18" leader, 3/0 Owner SSW; rig a 5" Big Hammer or MS Slug and drag it slow along the sand. Or flyline a live smelt/anchovy. The eat is a soft tap then dead weight — sweep-set, don't jerk.
rigs.line: 15–20 lb Sufix braid → 15 lb Seaguar fluoro

rigs.name: Dropshot, live smelt
rigs.detail: Live smelt on a 1/0 Gamakatsu dropshot hook, 12" above a 3/8 oz weight; hover just off the bottom over sand flats. Deadly on bigger fish.

rigs.name: Lucky Craft Pointer 100 / jerkbait
rigs.detail: Lucky Craft Pointer 100SP or Rapala X-Rap in sardine or ghost minnow; twitch-pause along the bottom edge in harbors and bays over clean sand.

yellowfin-croaker.cast: Into troughs and gutters along sandy/rocky beaches & harbor shallows at night

rigs.name: Ghost shrimp on a light rig
rigs.detail: Live or fresh ghost shrimp on a #4 Gamakatsu Octopus hook with a 1/4–1/2 oz egg sinker. Very sensitive bite — use a stiff-tip rod and watch for the tap-tap-pull before setting.

rigs.name: Hi-low rig, mussels or clams
rigs.detail: Two Mustad #4 droppers baited with fresh blue mussel or razor clam along the first trough; productive from dusk through midnight.

rigs.name: Berkley Gulp! Sandworm
rigs.detail: 2" Gulp! Sandworm in natural or blood on a single #4 dropper; scent reaches fish the cast can't. Works when ghost shrimp are unavailable.

white-croaker.cast: Drop down off any pier or jetty; short or vertical presentations work best

rigs.name: Hayabusa sabiki, small hooks
rigs.detail: Size 8–10 Hayabusa sabiki baited with tiny pieces of squid or anchovy; drop to the bottom and jig lightly. Prolific biter that doubles as live bait for larger species.

rigs.name: Small Carolina rig, cut squid
rigs.detail: 1/4 oz slider, #6 Mustad beak hook, thin strip of squid dragged slowly on the sand bottom; also picks up perch and small bass on the same rod.

rigs.name: Berkley Gulp! or nightcrawler drop
rigs.detail: Small piece of Gulp! or half a nightcrawler on a #8 Gamakatsu hook with a split shot; effective all night from piers and jetties.

white-seabass.cast: Along kelp edges and channel drop-offs; slow presentations near bottom

rigs.name: Live squid, flylined
rigs.detail: The top WSB bait — hook through the mantle on a 4/0 Owner SSW and let it swim freely near the kelp edge or channel bottom at dawn with zero weight. No dropper, no sinker.
rigs.line: 40 lb Seaguar Premiam Max fluoro leader

rigs.name: Live mackerel or sardine
rigs.detail: Nose-hook on a 5/0–6/0 Owner SSW circle hook; slow-drift or motor-troll along kelp walls at 1–1.5 mph. Circle hooks give the fish time to turn.
rigs.line: 40 lb Sufix 832 braid → 40 lb Seaguar fluoro

rigs.name: MC Swimbait 8–10"
rigs.detail: Large MC Swimbait or Huddleston Deluxe on a 2–3 oz heavy swimbait head worked slowly along the bottom near kelp structure — most effective when live bait isn't available.
rigs.line: 50 lb Sufix braid → 40 lb Seaguar fluoro

california-scorpionfish.cast: Drop straight down over reef and rocky structure; no movement needed
california-scorpionfish.note: Venomous dorsal spines — handle with a wet rag or long-nose pliers. Excellent eating.

rigs.name: Bottom dropper, fresh squid or shrimp
rigs.detail: 1–2 oz bank sinker, #1–2/0 Owner SSW hook, fresh squid strip or ghost shrimp placed directly on the rocky bottom. Sculpin ambush — don't move it. Drop, feel the bottom, wait.

rigs.name: Shrimp fly on a dropper
rigs.detail: A small shrimp-pattern Grunt or shrimp fly on a 12" dropper above the weight; lightly lift-drop over the reef to trigger the strike.

rigs.name: Berkley Gulp! Shrimp, 3"
rigs.detail: 3" Gulp! Shrimp in natural or pink on a #1 Gamakatsu hook sitting on the rocks; holds scent longer than fresh bait in current.

blue-rockfish.cast: Mid-water over the kelp canopy; vertical jigging or drift bait works well

regs.season: Verify CDFW regs — seasonal and depth closures apply

rigs.name: Dropper loop rig, cut squid
rigs.detail: Two-hook Mustad dropper rig with strips of squid or anchovy; drift mid-water just above the kelp canopy. Blue rockfish school here and bite an unweighted bait on the fall.

rigs.name: Diamond jig or butterfly jig
rigs.detail: 1/2–1 oz Salas Christy II or Shimano Butterfly jig; drop to mid-water and jig steadily upward. Chrome and blue-white are go-to colors.

rigs.name: Z-Man SwimmerZ, 3–4"
rigs.detail: Z-Man 3" SwimmerZ on a 1/4–1/2 oz Gamakatsu round head; work it through the water column with a steady retrieve over the kelp canopy.
rigs.line: 15 lb Sufix braid → 15 lb Seaguar fluoro

rainbow-trout.cast: Near inlet streams, dam face & open water; troll or soak near the surface in winter
rainbow-trout.depth: Top 20 ft in cold weather; deeper in summer

regs.season: Stocked Nov–Apr at Irvine Lake; verify current schedule

rigs.name: Berkley PowerBait, sliding sinker
rigs.detail: 1/4 oz sliding egg sinker, 24" 6 lb Seaguar leader, #14 Berkley PowerBait treble hook with a ball of chartreuse or rainbow PowerBait floating just off the bottom near the inlet. Classic and deadly on planted fish.
rigs.line: 8 lb main → 6 lb Seaguar fluoro leader

rigs.name: Acme Kastmaster, trolled
rigs.detail: 1/4–3/8 oz gold or silver Acme Kastmaster trolled at 1.5–2 mph on a long line near the surface. Cover water to find the school — color can matter on pressured fish.

rigs.name: Nightcrawler under a Thill float
rigs.detail: Whole nightcrawler on a #6 Gamakatsu hook suspended 3–6 ft under a Thill float drifted near the inlet current. Most effective early morning in winter.

bluegill.cast: Along dock edges, reed beds & rocky shallows; short casts with ultra-light tackle

rigs.name: Float rig, cricket or waxworm
rigs.detail: Small Thill float 2–3 ft up on a #8 Gamakatsu hook with a live cricket or waxworm. Let it sit still and watch for the dip — the single most reliable bluegill setup.

rigs.name: Bobby Garland 1/32 oz jig
rigs.detail: Bobby Garland Baby Shad or Mo' Glo in white or chartreuse on a 1/32 oz jig head; twitch lightly through the water column near docks and brush piles.

rigs.name: Nightcrawler piece
rigs.detail: Small chunk of nightcrawler on a #10 Gamakatsu hook with a BB split shot; works all day for bluegill, and often picks up bass and crappie on the same rod.

striped-bass.cast: Deep structure near the dam, rocky points & channel bends at dawn/dusk

rigs.name: Large swimbait
rigs.detail: 6–9" Huddleston Deluxe or MC Swimbait on a 1.5–2 oz head; fish it s-l-o-w along the bottom near rocky structure and channel bends. Stripers are ambush feeders — they don't chase fast retrieves.
rigs.line: 30–40 lb Sufix 832 braid → 25 lb Seaguar fluoro

rigs.name: Live crawfish or mudsucker
rigs.detail: Hooked through the tail on a 3/0 Owner SSW with a 1/2 oz sliding sinker; incredibly effective near rocky bottom. Mudsuckers (staghorn sculpin) are the top live bait.

rigs.name: Cut mackerel, bottom rig
rigs.detail: Fresh-cut mackerel on a 2/0 Owner circle hook, 1–2 oz sinker, soaked on the bottom at known striper haunts. Stripers nose-down at night — circle hooks make catch-and-release easy.
